violations.text: (Various food product ), which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. PIC voluntarily disposed of food product. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of (PIC ), (Various food product) was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep.PIC informed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2025
Birnia meat, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/27/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Birnia meat was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers covered with plastic wrap and lid. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/27/2025

Desserts and beverages packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. PIC started to label product with ingredients in Spanish. PIC was informed that label should also be in English. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the deli slicer have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. PIC took out of service to clean and sanitize. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/27/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (meat chopper, ice machine, and blender) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC notified. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Vegetable soup, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Various items in a prep unit were observed held between 47-48) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC voluntarily disposed of food product. PIC relocated other TCS foods into another unit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. PIC labeled. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Vegetable soup was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Various items packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025
Non-food grade containers were observed storing chips and dried peppers. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025
The hand sink in the kitchen area is lacking a splash guard. New Violation. Correct By: 02/27/2025

Knives were observed stored between the prep unit and prep table. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. PIC removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025
Cleaned and sanitized equipment and utensils are not stored inverted. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Wood is used in the construction of a platform stool in the kitchen area area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 02/27/2025
Prep unit located in the kitchen area was not in good repair. Ambient temperatures observed at 47 df. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the deli slicer and blender base have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

A hose is connected to the ice machine which is draining and stored in a bucket. This ice machine is lacking a backflow prevention device. An approved backflow prevention device is required in order to prevent backflow of a solid, liquid, or gas contaminant into the water supply at each point of use at a food establishment. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025
Basement ceiling tiles were observed in poor repair (water damage). Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2025
Hood vents were observed with an accumulation of grease and debris. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/11/2024
The ceiling in the basement area is covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/04/2024

Raw animal foods (eggs) were observed stored above (beef) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/04/2024
Birria, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/04/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of owner, birria was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. PIC disposed of. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/04/2024
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 01/11/2024
The door to the basement toilet room is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 01/11/2024
The ceiling in the basement area is covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. New Violation. Correct By: 01/04/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the ( ) area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 01/04/2024

An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. New Violation. Correct By: 12/28/2023

Raw animal foods (eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (vegetables, sauces) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/28/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Rust observed on blade and silicone missing. At the time of inspection, PIC took out of service. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/28/2023

Steak and birria, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. PIC disposed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/28/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of employee, prepared meats are left out over night in containers greater than four inches deep. New Violation. Correct By: 12/28/2023

Knives were stored on an unclean surface (behind 3 bay sink) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/28/2023
At the time of inspection, dishes were stored in mop sink. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/28/2023
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 01/11/2024
The door to the basement toilet room is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 12/28/2023
The ceiling in the basement area is covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. New Violation. Correct By: 01/25/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 01/11/2024
